title: Realm Fables - Part 2 - More Tools for Generating Your World (especially a library)
body:
In this video, Daniel follows up on his earlier exploration of the Realm Fables Kickstarter, focusing on the remaining two components: the Miscellany and Preformed Settings Volume 1. He begins by sharing his recommendations, suggesting that the most valuable parts for solo role-playing are the World Generator Volumes 1 and 2 and the Sandbox Creator. While Miscellany and Preformed Settings offer interesting content, he doesn’t find them as immediately useful for solo role-play, with one notable exception being the villain creation section.

Daniel walks through the Miscellany book, which includes sections on generating hex worlds, sanctuaries for adventurers, and a detailed system for creating libraries—though he finds the focus on libraries somewhat excessive. The book also contains valuable tables for building strong villains, with features like memorable traits, items, and backstories that can help solo players create more complex and emotionally engaging antagonists.

Next, he explores the Preformed Settings Volume 1, which includes two micro-settings, Salt and Rhyme and Shadow and Ash. These pre-built worlds offer ready-to-use settings complete with NPCs, locations, rumors, and encounters, making them a solid starting point for sandbox-style adventures. Daniel expresses interest in using these settings, especially Salt and Rhyme, for future solo adventures in games like Scarlet Heroes.

In conclusion, Daniel highlights the potential of these tools for enriching solo RPGs, emphasizing the villain generation tools and the settings’ potential for fleshing out narratives and world-building.

https://www.drivethrurpg.com/en/product/342692/realm-fables-world-generator
https://www.drivethrurpg.com/en/product/397039/realm-fables-world-generator-volume-2

Realm Fables - Part 1 - Tools for Generating Your World The Channel
[Realm Fables - Part 1 - Tools for Generating Your World] In this video, Daniel introduces Realm Fables and the Sandbox Creator, a series of books designed to help create detailed world maps, cities, and settings for solo skirmish and narrative-style games like Five Leagues from the Borderlands. The Sandbox Creator helps you build a landmass by using dice, cards, and tables to create different geographical features, points of interest, and connections between areas. Daniel demonstra…
